steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the subject of the verb ‘correlate.’ Two things must be right: (1) the subject must be plural to agree with ‘correlate,’ and (2) the noun must show that the lengths belong to the constitutions, so a possessive is required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'constitutions’ length,': the possessive form is correct (plural possessive), but ‘length’ is singular. A singular subject cannot agree with the plural verb ‘correlate.’ ✗
  • Choice B — 'constitutions lengths,': no apostrophe at all, so there is no possessive. The sentence would claim that ‘constitutions lengths’ are a thing — two nouns with no relationship marked. ✗
  • Choice C — 'constitution’s length,': singular possessive (one constitution’s) and singular noun (length). Both the possessive and the number are wrong — the passage refers to two constitutions with lengths that ‘correlate.’ ✗
  • Choice D — 'constitutions’ lengths,': plural possessive (constitutions’) correctly attributes the lengths to multiple constitutions, and ‘lengths’ is plural, agreeing with ‘correlate.’ Both requirements are met. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
